mnemonics: Fix The Blurry Picture: Degrade -> Noise -> Reconstruct -> Restore
keyPoints: Image restoration aims to recover an original image from its degraded version using a model of the degradation and noise.
Reconstruction often refers to creating a 2D image or 3D volume from a set of 1D projections, as in Computed Tomography (CT).
The key distinction is that restoration works from a degraded image, while reconstruction builds an image from non-image data.
commonMistakes: Confusing Image Restoration (fixing an existing image) with Image Reconstruction (building an image from other data).
Assuming all noise is Gaussian; different types (e.g., salt-and-pepper, periodic) require different restoration filters.
